companies.name: Abacus Insights
companies.summary: BlueBook 2025 interview entries for Abacus Insights.

questions.text: Why did you choose the Python programming language for Data Science?
Questions on NumPy and Pandas: What's the use of NumPy and Pandas libraries?
Do you know the difference between iloc and loc in Pandas?
We usually don't use Pandas when we have to host the web server as it isn't well suited for web servers. What alternatives do we use then?
Why can't we use lists instead of NumPy arrays?
Does NumPy internally create Python lists itself?
Why are NumPy arrays faster than Python lists?
Are NumPy arrays mutable?
Question about pickle library : Do you know about pickle in Python?
Why not R?
What's the difference between the list and array?
Do you know about the concept of mutability?
Have you ever built a web server?
What web server did you use?
What is a Jupyter notebook?
Is SQLite a database?
Suppose we have a pickle file of a model that is trained on 1TB of data. Now, we get 4TBs more data. Is the pickle file we already have useful in such a scenario?

questions.text: Assessment tasks included problem-solving and coding
Questions about computer vision applications in sports

Feedback received was positive Questions from interview - (18 & 24) December 2025: Explain CNN including concepts like stride, pooling, kernel with activation functions. Given an image of dimension 64×64×3 and kernel of dimension 3×3 with stride of
What will the dimension of the resulting matrix be and the total number of parameters without bias?
Project-based questions

What will the dimension of the resulting matrix be? What metrics are considered for evaluation while training an object detection model like YOLO? What is the input format for the YOLOv8 model? Explain in brief about RCNN and its variants. Question on projects - Which version of yolo did you use. Explain about XGBoost. What is the formula for F1 score? At the end of the interview, a task was assigned, on the basis of which further process will take place. The task is to train YOLOv11n and YOLOv11s on the cricket video frames and share the results with the evaluator via mail. Questions from interview - 2/6/2026 Project Project explanation and complete walkthrough of the implemented pipeline. Which YOLO model was used in the project, and whether the model was fine-tuned or used as a pre-trained model. Questions about the tools, models, and techniques used in the project, including the choice of YOLO version, how YOLO works, how DeepSORT integrates with YOLO for tracking, the role of the embedder, and related deep learning concepts. Why did you choose YOLOv8s for object detection? Why did you use Bytetrack for tracking players? What is the role of an activation function in neural networks? Which activation functions are commonly used in CNNs? Explain gradient descent and backpropagation, and how weights and biases are updated during training. Compare between Cross-Entropy loss and Mean Squared Error (MSE), and under which circumstances should they be used. Numerical questions based on convolution output size calculation given input image size, kernel size, padding, and stride (including variations such as stride = 0, stride = 1, and stride = 2). Follow-up numerical questions on calculating the number of parameters in a convolutional layer. Explanation of how YOLO works and how YOLO and DeepSORT are integrated for detection and tracking. Why YOLO alone cannot be used for tracking and why an additional tracking algorithm like DeepSORT is required. Question on how object detection labels should be structured, specifically the annotation format used by YOLO What are the main challenges in detecting players in cricket videos (e.g., motion blur, small objects, occlusion, similar background), and how would you handle them? companies.name: KPI Partners Interview
tags: Data Engineer Intern Data Engineer (Employment) Data Engineer – Intern + PPO Project Trainee (Probation) → Data Engineer 1 (Full-Time)

Internship + Employment Mode of Interview: Online Number of Rounds: 2–3 (Technical, Coding, Project Discussion, Managerial) 1. Technical Evaluation Core Topics Assessed Across Candidates Interviewers consistently focused on the following areas: SQL (intermediate to advanced) Window functions, grouping, joins, schema-based queries, and scenario-based SQL logic. Python (easy to medium coding) String manipulation, dictionaries, task optimization logic, and pandas operations. Database Concepts Indexing, hashing, B-trees, normalization, schema design, data modeling. Data Engineering Fundamentals ETL concepts, data warehousing basics, log-tracking systems, comparison of data engineering vs. data science. Modern Frameworks & Tools PySpark, LangChain, AI agent workflows, RAG-based systems, Redis, Celery, project pipeline explanations. Project Deep Dives Detailed questions on past internships, ETL design, pipeline failures, and end-to-end data flow. 2. Coding, SQL & Practical Assessments Common Coding & SQL Tasks Across Candidates Candidates were asked to solve real-world engineering problems such as: SQL Tasks: Find top employees per department. Design fact/dimension tables for reward systems. Querying vehicle tables with customized ordering (Truck → Car → Bike). Airport incoming/outgoing flight counts. Consecutive month product sales without using WHERE clause. Generate category IDs. Medium–advanced SQL problem solving from scratch. Python Tasks: First non-repeating character. Average waiting time calculations. String occurrence counts. Aggregation and dictionary-based logic. Custom logic tasks (e.g., computing output from lists of strings, team-balancing tasks). Pandas Tasks: Filtering, transformations, and schema-based operations. Schema Design: Designing transactional logs, login attempt tracking systems, reward systems, and shop/order schemas. Task Complexity: Easy → Intermediate → Advanced, depending on role. Most candidates reported the tasks as comfortable or moderately challenging. 3. Behavioral & Managerial Discussion Behavioral questioning was light to moderate depending on the interviewer. Common questions included: Why Data Engineering? Preference for working in a team vs. independently. Handling challenges and real project blockers. Time management scenarios. Personal introduction unrelated to technical work. Candidates answered based on past experience and academic projects. 4. Project Discussion & Tool Explanation Almost all candidates underwent deep dives into their projects, covering: ETL pipeline implementation Use of PySpark, Redis, Celery Data orchestration RAG systems and LangGraph workflows How LLMs were integrated or switched in projects SQL-heavy backend logic and schema decisions Candidates who had strong resumes with detailed projects were questioned extensively, often asked to share PPTs, workflows, and architecture diagrams. 5. Feedback, Communication & Candidate Comfort Interview flow was smooth and well-structured for most candidates. Next steps were clearly communicated. Many were told they had strong technical skills. Direct feedback was given in some cases; others received general appreciation without specifics. Most candidates reported being comfortable throughout the interview, even during difficult SQL rounds. Job Role - Data Engineer Interview Date - 2/4/2026, 2/3/2026 Job Type - Employment Job Domain - Data Science, AI

questions.text: "Data Engineering & SQL Difference between window functions and aggregate functions Write SQL query to find the second highest student marks using DENSE_RANK / ROW_NUMBER GROUP BY vs window functions How do you build ETL pipelines How can we handle non-relational databases? How do you use python and SQL for automation and data processing Python & AI Write a Python program to print first 10/20 prime numbers Explain list comprehension What are decorators? What are lambda (inline) functions? Python packages used in projects Machine Learning / AI What is an LLM? Explain Transformers architecture Drawbacks of Transformers Training vs Fine-tuning What is Transfer Learning? What is chunking and tokenization? Types of similarity measures (cosine, euclidean, etc.) LLM hyperparameters (temperature, top-p, max tokens) How to handle hallucinations in LLMs How to reverse the string without using the inbuilt function? One dictionary related problem. Projects What problems do you face during the development of a project? How do you go about solving the problem. Questions on tools and techniques used Explain RAG architecture that i used in my project Explain some of the ML models used in your project Tech stack explanation (backend, database, cloud) The difference between relational and dimensional models, and how your project data was structured for querying and analysis. Have you worked on any llm based project? Questions on why certain technologies were used What are the core foundations of data engineering? What was your source data in your recent project, and what validations or cleaning did you perform? You receive a student file with ID, name, phone number, and email — how would you validate that the phone number is correctly formatted and not blank? Files arrive in an AWS S3 bucket every 5 minutes — how would you design a pipeline to load data into a database, reject invalid files, send notifications, and continue processing? Design a simple relational data model with 3–4 tables for your project, and write a SQL query to find the top 3 users who asked the most queries in January

Define the sorting and searching algorithm. Do you know about confusion matrix? What are the evaluation matrix we use in regression model? If our data is imbalanced then which evaluation metrics we use? He ask me about how do you handle the duplicate user registration in web application project. Coding Question : Write a Python validation function to check phone number formats. The candidate was asked to design a simple AWS S3 ingestion pipeline logic with validation and rejection handling. Writing SQL queries on a sample relational data model, such as finding the top users by query count, identifying missing responses using joins, and doing weekly aggregation of failed queries. Write a Python program to print first k prime numbers. Create is_prime() function use loop + square root optimization SQL : find second highest student marks with name, can solved using DENSE_RANK() window function Preprocessing files using AWS S3 SQL-based problems on a relational data model such as ranking users, handling missing records with joins, and doing week-wise aggregation of query failures. Other questions: How do you manage your part time work with studies How did you handle real projects during internships What was your approach when you were independently learning RAG embeddings? APIs, etc. What experience did you gain during your internship?

companies.summary: BlueBook 2025 interview entries for LatentView Analytics Interview.

rounds.name: Technical + M anagerial Round 1 (Technical – M achine Learning & Analytics Concepts) The interviewer asked questions related to core machine learning models and their applications in analytics-driven environments. Key areas discussed: Machine Learning model selection for analytics tasks Understanding of regression, classification, and evaluation metrics Scenario-based questions on model interpretation Discussion on previous projects and model implementation strategies The candidate was also asked to comment on specific algorithms, their working, and how they handled real datasets during their project work. Round 2 (M anagerial Discussion) The managerial round included: Understanding the candidate’s approach to problem-solving Ability to explain reasoning behind model choices Communication ability and clarity of thought Ensuring alignment with data-driven decision-making expectations Coding / Tasks: The interviewer asked problem-based M L questions, but no live coding tasks were given. Behavioral Questions: Yes, the candidate was asked situational questions and responded confidently, highlighting clarity in decision-making and technical communication. Company : latentview Analytics Role 2 : AI Engineer Apprentice (Internship) Interview Rounds: Technical, M anagerial, HR Round 1 (Technical – M L, DL, Agentic AI Concepts) The interview began with a deep dive into theoretical and applied AI concepts. Key topics covered: Bias–Variance Tradeoff ML and DL fundamentals Scenario-based problem discussions Agentic AI concepts relevant to the role Understanding of ML pipelines and decision points The candidate was asked to explain thought processes behind various models, hyperparameter choices, and specific techniques used in their projects. Coding / W hiteboard Tasks: No live coding problems were asked. However, the interviewer tested depth of understanding in ML & DL by asking advanced conceptual questions. Round 2 (Technical – Project & Implementation Deep Dive) This round focused heavily on: RAG projects implemented by the candidate Agentic system design and evaluation Hyperparameters tuning strategies Testing inference stages and ensuring reliability of GenAI systems Implementation details of a voice assistant using GenAI The interviewer explored the complete architecture and reasoning behind design choices. Round 3 (M anagerial Discussion) Managerial questions included: Handling situational/behavioral scenarios Decision-making process during model development Communication clarity and confidence The candidate handled these questions using knowledge from the Deep Learning course and prior projects. Round 4 (HR Discussion) This round included: Clarity on selection process Explanation of next steps Final alignment with company expectations Company : Latentview Analytics Role 3 : Data M odeling (Internship) Rounds : Technical, Coding, HR
